title: EPIC: Implement Chacana DSL and Grammar
docs: fix LaTeX transpiler positioning in API docs and navigation
description:
Source: Documentation review 2026-04-23 ([INTENT-002], [ACC-001]).

Traceability:
- Derived from the 2026-04-23 documentation review recorded in wai research.

Context:
- docs/api/latex.md appears in the Python API nav, but the LaTeX transpiler is currently implemented in the TypeScript/browser engine rather than src/chacana/.
- This creates a trust and accuracy problem for readers expecting Python package support.

Required direction:
- Remove the implication that this is part of the Python package API.
- Either move/rehome the page into a browser/editor-focused section, or relabel it clearly outside the Python API reference.

Files:
- mkdocs.yml
- docs/api/latex.md
- docs/api/index.md
- README.md and/or docs/index.md if cross-links mention availability

Acceptance criteria:
- Navigation and page labeling no longer imply Python package availability if that is not true
- The page clearly states where the feature exists today and where it does not
- Any references from landing pages or guide pages are updated to match
- Source claims are verified against implementation before merge
- Docs build passes

Verification:
- `uv run mkdocs build`
- `rg -n "fromLatex|toLatex|latex" src vscode-chacana docs -g '!docs/assets/js/chacana-checker.js'`
- Manually inspect site navigation and the rendered LaTeX page wording

Workflow mandate:
- Start from the failing trust/accuracy case, verify implementation boundaries in code, then update wording/navigation, then tidy adjacent references.

title: refactor: make parse() always call check() regardless of context presence
description:
In __init__.py:42-43, check(token, context) is only called when context is not None. But check() itself gracefully handles ctx=None and still runs contraction (Rule 1), free-index invariance (Rule 2), and symmetry (Rule 3) checks. The current gate means chacana.parse("A{_a} * B{_a}") silently accepts a same-variance contraction error.

The fix is simple: always call check(token, context) unconditionally. The check() function already handles None context correctly — it skips rank and operator checks but still validates the structural rules.

This single change:
1. Fixes chacana-3xr (parse skips checks)
2. Makes chacana-9bi tests meaningful (they currently test "no context = no checks")
3. Aligns the public API with the checker's actual capabilities
4. Is backwards-compatible since check() doesn't require context

File: src/chacana/__init__.py (1-line change)
Subsumes: chacana-3xr
Unblocks: chacana-9bi (which can then be fixed to add meaningful assertions)

title: refactor: extract IndexAnalyzer to unify index traversal and fix metric contraction
description:
Five functions independently traverse/manipulate indices: `_free_indices`, `_remove_contracted`, `_get_all_indices`, contraction validation in `_check_contraction`, and symmetry validation in `_check_symmetry`. They duplicate logic and have inconsistent handling of context (metric awareness).

Extract an `IndexAnalyzer` class that:
1. Takes a ValidationToken and optional GlobalContext
2. Provides `free_indices(token) -> list[ChacanaIndex]` that IS metric-aware (fixes chacana-6si)
3. Provides `all_indices(token) -> list[ChacanaIndex]` that returns the CORRECT free indices of Add nodes, not just the first arg (fixes chacana-y74)
4. Provides `contracted_pairs(token) -> list[tuple[ChacanaIndex, ChacanaIndex]]` for explicit pair tracking
5. Handles wedge products correctly — either validate or explicitly document that wedge skips contraction (fixes chacana-8gv)
6. Centralizes the remove_contracted algorithm with metric-awareness as a parameter

The key architectural insight: `_free_indices` and `_remove_contracted` need access to `ctx.active_metric` but currently don't receive it. Thread the context through.

Files: src/chacana/checker.py (extract to new src/chacana/indices.py), vscode-chacana/src/server/checker.ts
Subsumes: chacana-6si, chacana-y74, chacana-8gv
close_reason: Extracted IndexAnalyzer, fixed all three bugs in commit 1a1da30

title: refactor: replace untyped metadata dict with typed TokenMetadata dataclass
description:
`ValidationToken.metadata` is `dict[str, Any]` with hardcoded string keys ("symmetrized_groups", "antisymmetrized_groups", "order"). The visitor writes these keys, the checker reads them — coupling via string names with no compile-time safety.

Replace with a typed dataclass:
```python
@dataclass
class TokenMetadata:
    symmetrized_groups: list[list[int]] = field(default_factory=list)
    antisymmetrized_groups: list[list[int]] = field(default_factory=list)
    order: int | None = None
```

This change:
1. Makes the coupling explicit and type-safe
2. Enables bounds validation at construction time (fixes chacana-kpa)
3. Forces visit_factor to unpack tuple properly since the type won't accept it (fixes chacana-nen)
4. Eliminates string-key typo risk
5. Simplifies to_dict() serialization

Files: src/chacana/ast.py, src/chacana/visitor.py, src/chacana/checker.py, vscode-chacana/src/server/ast.ts, vscode-chacana/src/server/astBuilder.ts, vscode-chacana/src/server/checker.ts
Subsumes: chacana-kpa (bounds check), chacana-nen (tuple vs list)

title: refactor: extract TokenWalker to replace 10+ manual recursion loops in checker
description:
The checker has 10+ manual `for arg in token.args` recursion loops across `_check_contraction`, `_check_free_index_invariance`, `_check_symmetry`, `_check_rank`, `_check_operators`, `_free_indices`, `_get_all_indices`, and `_remove_contracted`. Each function independently implements the same head-based dispatch pattern (check HEAD_ADD, HEAD_NEGATE, HEAD_MULTIPLY, HEAD_WEDGE, etc. then recurse into args).

Extract a `TokenWalker` base class or utility that:
1. Provides pre-order and post-order traversal of ValidationToken trees
2. Supports head-based dispatch via method overrides or a visitor dict
3. Handles the "recurse into args" pattern once, with hooks for collect-then-validate
4. Explicitly handles HEAD_NEGATE instead of relying on accidental fallthrough (fixes chacana-2df)

This applies to BOTH checker.py and checker.ts (which is a direct port).

Files: src/chacana/checker.py, vscode-chacana/src/server/checker.ts
Subsumes: chacana-2df (negate fallthrough)
Ref: 10 recursion loops identified in architectural review

title: Architectural refactoring: simplify abstractions across codebase
description: Epic tracking refactoring proposals identified during architectural review. Each refactoring addresses a cluster of red-team bugs by introducing better abstractions rather than point-fixing individual issues. These refactorings should be done BEFORE or INSTEAD OF individual bug fixes where noted.

title: Nested symmetrization accepted by grammar, violating spec requirement to reject
description: The _index_element rule in grammar.js allows symmetrization/anti_symmetrization as choices, and the index_list inside symmetrization uses the same _index_element rule. This means T{_( _( a b _) _)} successfully parses. The PEG spec explicitly states 'the parser MUST reject recursive symmetrization.' Need a separate _sym_index_element that only allows $.index.
notes: Ref: openspec/specs/peg-grammar/spec.md — section on nested symmetrization rejection

title: Line-by-line parsing makes multi-line expressions impossible
description: server.ts:78-120 splits the document by newlines and parses each line independently. Multi-line expressions are fundamentally impossible. All AST SourceRange positions have startLine:0/endLine:0 from single-line parsing, requiring manual line number override. This architecture severely limits the language server.
notes: SCOPE: This is an architectural redesign, not a simple bug fix. Requires a design phase before implementation. Consider splitting into sub-tasks: (1) multi-expression grammar support, (2) document-level parsing strategy, (3) tree storage rewrite, (4) position mapping update, (5) hover/definition update.
close_reason: Replaced line-by-line parsing with whole-document parsing. Updated WASM to match current grammar. Fixed hover for operator_keyword nodes.

title: Memory leak — per-line tree objects accumulate and cleanup uses wrong key format
description: In server.ts:85, trees are stored with keys ${doc.uri}:${lineIdx}. But onDidClose (line 67) calls trees.delete(event.document.uri) which is just the URI without the :lineIdx suffix — this key never exists in the map, so the delete is a no-op. Trees accumulate indefinitely. When a file shrinks (lines deleted), stale entries for higher line numbers also persist forever.
close_reason: Fixed by chacana-ylp in commit d440315 (LSP tree storage redesign)

title: Non-null assertion on node.parent crashes hover/definition providers on root nodes
description: Both hover.ts:34 and definition.ts:31 use node = node.parent! in a while loop. If descendantForPosition returns a root-level anonymous node, node.parent is null, the non-null assertion lies, and the next iteration dereferences null.isNamed causing a crash. The LSP server would fail silently for hover/go-to-definition on certain edge-case positions.

title: parse() skips ALL type checking when context=None, silently accepting invalid expressions
description: In __init__.py:42-43, check(token, context) is only called when context is not None. But check() itself handles ctx=None gracefully and still runs contraction, free-index, and symmetry checks. This means chacana.parse("A{_a} * B{_a}") (same-variance contraction error) returns a valid dict with no error when no context is provided.

title: _free_indices and _remove_contracted unaware of metric-aware contraction
description: When ctx.active_metric is set, _check_contraction correctly allows same-variance contraction (checker.py:111-113). But _free_indices/_remove_contracted (checker.py:67-89) only remove pairs with opposite variance, so same-variance contracted indices still appear as "free". This breaks _check_free_index_invariance for any sum containing metric contractions. E.g., A{_a} * B{_a} + C with active metric should be valid but fails on free index mismatch.
close_reason: Extracted IndexAnalyzer, fixed all three bugs in commit 1a1da30

title: _get_all_indices for Add nodes only examines first arg, missing cross-term contraction errors
description: _get_all_indices (checker.py:129-131) returns only _get_all_indices(token.args[0]) for HEAD_ADD. When an Add is inside a Multiply, indices from later sum terms that interact with sibling tensors in the product are invisible to the contraction counter. E.g., (A{^a} + B{^a ^c}) * C{_a} * D{^c} — the ^c from B and D creates a same-variance error, but it's never detected because only A's indices are counted.
close_reason: Extracted IndexAnalyzer, fixed all three bugs in commit 1a1da30

title: Red-team code review: codebase-wide bug audit
description: Epic tracking all bugs found during comprehensive red-team review of the entire Chacana codebase. Covers Python core, type checker, VS Code extension/LSP, tree-sitter grammar, test suite, docs, and build configuration.
close_reason: All 37 sub-issues resolved — red-team audit complete

title: EPIC: Implement processor interface, tequitl, and yachay
description: Implementation epic for the three new capabilities: processor-interface (conformance tests, schema validation), tequitl (Julia processor with Symbolics.jl, juliac, AD surfaces), and yachay (invariant database with TOML+Chacana format). Spec: openspec/specs/{processor-interface,tequitl-architecture,yachay-data-model}/spec.md. Cross-ref: sxAct-9yo8 (integration epic).

title: EPIC: Chacana-sxAct integration
description: Umbrella epic tracking all work needed to connect chacana's parser/checker to sxAct's translate tooling, adapter protocol, and three-tier verification pipeline. Milestones: (1) Stabilize all specs, (2) Publish to PyPI, (3) sxAct consumes chacana as dependency, (4) Translate CLI accepts --from chacana, (5) ChacanaAdapter in sxact, (6) Tier 3 Chacana-jl backend.
notes: New specs applied: processor-interface, tequitl-architecture, yachay-data-model are now canonical in openspec/specs/. Implementation epic to be created. Deferred spec updates (5.1-5.3) complete.

title: Stabilize PEG grammar spec to v1.0
description: Move PEG grammar specification from DRAFT v0.2.4 to STABLE v1.0. The Arpeggio implementation fully covers the spec. Required before Tree-sitter implementation (chacana-wym.4) and sxAct translate integration (sxAct-u834). Scope: verify grammar rules match implementation, update version, remove DRAFT.
notes: Cross-ref: sxAct-u834 (translate tooling) and chacana-wym.4 (Tree-sitter) are blocked until this is STABLE.
close_reason: PEG grammar spec stabilized to v1.0.0: grammar rules aligned with Arpeggio implementation (6 fixes), functional operator index attachment scenario added. All 93 tests pass.

title: Stabilize DSL specification to v1.0
description: Move DSL specification from DRAFT v0.2.2 to STABLE v1.0. The implementation (parser, visitor, checker) is complete and tested. Spec finalization unblocks all downstream consumers in sxAct: translate tooling (sxAct-u834), adapter integration, and Tier 3 design. Scope: review spec against implementation, resolve any gaps, remove DRAFT status, tag v1.0.
notes: Cross-ref: sxAct-u834 and sxAct-24k5 are blocked until this is STABLE.
close_reason: DSL spec stabilized to v1.0.0: 25 new scenarios added covering contraction rules, rank/variance validation, operator constraints, context validation. All 121 tests pass.

title: Fix all RO5U review findings
description: Fix 2 HIGH, 6 MEDIUM, 2 LOW issues: subtraction semantics, symmetrization metadata, d() crash, head constants, bare index default, context.py guards, parser caching, set→multiset comparison.
close_reason: All 10 RO5U findings fixed: subtraction semantics (Negate), symmetrization metadata, d() crash guard, head constants, parser caching, Counter comparison, context error wrapping. 220 tests.

title: Add Sphinx/mkdocs documentation with GitHub Pages deployment
description: Set up proper API documentation site using mkdocs-material with autodoc, deploy to GitHub Pages via CI.
close_reason: mkdocs-material site with autodoc API reference, user guide, GitHub Pages deployment workflow. Builds clean with --strict.

title: Configure dev tooling: pre-commit, pre-push, CI, docs
description: Set up ruff (lint+format), pre-commit hooks, pre-push test runner, GitHub Actions CI, and minimal API docs.
close_reason: All tooling configured: ruff (lint+format), mypy (typecheck), typos (spell check), vale (prose lint), git hooks (pre-commit + pre-push), GitHub Actions CI (lint + test matrix 3.10-3.13). README updated.

title: Tracer bullet: end-to-end DSL parse→check pipeline
description: Implement thinnest possible path: TOML context + expression string → PEG parse → MathJSON AST → type check → validated token. Proves architecture with working parse('R{^a _b _c _d}').
close_reason: All 7 steps implemented. 50 tests passing. Full pipeline: TOML→parse→AST→check→output working.

title: Implement Differential Geometry Operations (Exterior Calculus, Lie Derivative)
description: Implement the core logic for the exterior derivative (d), Hodge star (*), interior product (i), and Lie derivative (L) in the processor. MUST support coordinate-free symbolic manipulation as per openspec/specs/differential-geometry/spec.md.
close_reason: All diff geometry operators type-checked: Hodge star, interior product, Lie derivative, trace, determinant, inverse. 45 tests.

title: TASK: Implement TOML Context (Γ) Parser
description: Implement the parser for the TOML-based Global Context (Γ) for defining manifolds, tensors, and metrics.
close_reason: Full TOML context parser with cross-referential validation, sparsity/perturbation sections, strategy preservation. 29 context tests.

title: TASK: Implement Static Index Type Checker
description: Implement the Static Index Type Checker to validate variance consistency, contraction rules, and exterior calculus rank propagation. MUST enforce negative path checks for mismatched indices and invalid operator applications. Ref: openspec/specs/dsl-specification/spec.md
close_reason: All 3 spec rules implemented: contraction with index_type, free index invariance, symmetry validation. Full recursion into all node types. 34 checker tests.

title: TASK: Implement Chacana PEG Grammar (Python/Arpeggio)
description: Implement the deterministic PEG grammar for the Chacana micro-syntax using Python and Arpeggio. MUST support functional operators (d, L, Tr, det, inv), explicit symmetrization, and Unicode/Rational normalization. Ref: openspec/specs/peg-grammar/spec.md
close_reason: Full spec coverage: all grammar rules, Unicode NFC normalization, Cyrillic/CJK rejection, nested symmetrization rejection. 102 tests.

title: Playground: add CI step to sync checker bundle to docs
description: The docs/assets/js/chacana-checker.js was 4KB behind vscode-chacana/dist/browser/chacana-checker.js, causing toLatex and fromLatex to be missing. Add a CI check or build step to ensure the docs copy stays in sync with the dist bundle.

title: fromLatex: convert \nabla to covariant derivative syntax
description: fromLatex passes through `\nabla_e` literally instead of converting to Chacana covariant derivative syntax (`;e` suffix). E.g. `R^{a}_{bcd} \nabla_e T^{bc}` should become `R{^a _b _c _d} * T{^b ^c ;e}` or similar.

title: fromLatex: insert explicit * between adjacent tensor products
description: fromLatex outputs juxtaposed tensors like `R{^a _b _c _d} g{^b ^d}` but Chacana requires explicit `*` operator: `R{^a _b _c _d} * g{^b ^d}`. The parser rejects implicit multiplication, causing syntax errors when importing valid LaTeX.

title: docs: add troubleshooting tables for type checking and differential geometry
description:
Source: Documentation review 2026-04-23 ([SCAN-002]).

Traceability:
- Derived from the 2026-04-23 documentation review recorded in wai research.

Context:
- docs/guide/type-checking.md and docs/guide/diffgeom.md explain rules and operators, but they are not optimized for debugging.
- Readers need scan-friendly mappings from error/fail-state to likely cause and fix.

Files:
- docs/guide/type-checking.md
- docs/guide/diffgeom.md

Acceptance criteria:
- Add error/fail-state tables or equivalent scan-friendly sections to both pages
- Include minimal examples for common failures and their fixes
- Keep examples consistent with current checker behavior
- Scope is limited to troubleshooting and debugging-oriented improvements on these two pages
- Docs build passes

Verification:
- `uv run mkdocs build`
- Manually verify each page includes an error/fail-state mapping with example and fix
- Manually check examples against current documented checker/operator behavior

Workflow mandate:
- Identify the main failing reader debugging scenarios first, then add tables/examples to resolve them, then tidy wording and layout separately.

title: docs: restructure editor support page for scanability and standalone use
description:
Source: Documentation review 2026-04-23 ([SCAN-001], [EPPO-002]).

Traceability:
- Derived from the 2026-04-23 documentation review recorded in wai research.

Context:
- docs/guide/editor-support.md contains useful details, but setup paths and capability differences are buried in dense prose.
- Readers landing here should be able to see supported editors, installation routes, context resolution, and limitations within seconds.

Files:
- docs/guide/editor-support.md
- docs/playground.md (only if cross-links or framing need alignment with editor-support claims)

Acceptance criteria:
- Add answer-first framing and prerequisites/context in the opening section
- Reformat capabilities and setup into scan-friendly lists/tables/checklists
- Summarize context resolution and no-context behavior clearly
- Ensure playground cross-links and editor claims are accurate
- Keep scope centered on editor-support structure; broader heading/TL;DR cleanup on other pages belongs elsewhere
- Docs build passes

Verification:
- `uv run mkdocs build`
- Manually verify a reader can identify supported editors, install path, and context-resolution behavior in under a minute
- Manually verify any playground cross-links and editor claims against current repo contents

Workflow mandate:
- Treat fast reader foraging as the failing case, then restructure for scanning, then tidy wording/formatting separately.

title: docs: make installation and quickstart standalone and whole-game oriented
description:
Source: Documentation review 2026-04-23 ([EPPO-001], [ACC-002], [EXCL-001]).

Traceability:
- Derived from the 2026-04-23 documentation review recorded in wai research.

Context:
- docs/getting-started/installation.md and docs/getting-started/quickstart.md need stronger standalone framing.
- Use one canonical whole-game path so readers can succeed without interpretation.

Canonical reader journey:
1. Install with `uv sync --dev`
2. Verify with `python -c "import chacana; print(chacana.__version__)"`
3. Load `examples/basic.toml`
4. Parse one valid expression
5. Show one invalid expression raising `ChacanaTypeError`

Files:
- docs/getting-started/installation.md
- docs/getting-started/quickstart.md
- examples/basic.toml (if example alignment is needed)

Acceptance criteria:
- Each page begins with a short TL;DR/prerequisites block
- Quickstart presents the canonical runnable whole-game example above
- Installation page includes a clear verification step and what success looks like
- Cross-links between installation and quickstart are explicit and correct
- Docs build passes

Verification:
- `uv run mkdocs build`
- Manually verify the quickstart follows the canonical 5-step path
- Manually verify commands/examples match repository files and current public API

Workflow mandate:
- Define the successful first-run experience first, then revise the docs to match it, then perform wording/formatting cleanup separately.

title: docs: clarify homepage and README intent, audience, and next steps
description:
Source: Documentation review 2026-04-23 ([INTENT-001], [ACC-002], [EXCL-001]).

Traceability:
- Derived from the 2026-04-23 documentation review recorded in wai research.

Context:
- README.md and docs/index.md currently mix landing-page, explanation, and reference intents.
- New readers do not get one answer-first path that says what Chacana is for, who it is for, and what to do next.
- This ticket should follow the LaTeX positioning fix so top-level capability messaging stays accurate.

Files:
- README.md
- docs/index.md

Acceptance criteria:
- Add answer-first opening sections with audience, capability summary, and clear next steps
- Separate landing-page content from deeper architecture/reference detail
- Include a concise whole-game path that points readers to installation, quickstart, and API/reference docs
- Keep scope limited to top-level messaging, openings, and next-step links; navigation or deeper guide rewrites belong in follow-up tickets

Verification:
- `uv run mkdocs build`
- Manually review README.md and docs/index.md openings for answer-first framing and accurate capability claims
- Manually confirm next-step links point to the intended pages

Workflow mandate:
- Treat the opening reader journey as the failing case, then revise content to satisfy it, then do any wording/structure cleanup in a tidy pass.

title: EPIC: Documentation IA and AI-readiness refresh
description:
Source: review-documentation audit dated 2026-04-23.

This epic tracks the documentation revisions needed to address issues found in the five-pass review: intent separation, standalone utility, cognitive scannability, accuracy, whole-game onboarding, and AI-readiness.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Landing pages clearly separate tutorial/how-to/reference/explanation intents
- [ ] Core docs include answer-first framing, prerequisites, and next steps
- [ ] Accuracy gaps and misleading API positioning are corrected
- [ ] Troubleshooting and scanability improvements are shipped for key guides
- [ ] All changes are verified by building docs with `uv run mkdocs build`
notes:
Workflow mandate:
- Follow TDD/Tidy First for documentation work: write or identify the failing reader journey/example first, then revise content, then do structure-only cleanup separately.
- Verify examples and navigation claims against the codebase before editing.

title: Implement Python to_latex / from_latex transpiler
description: Port the TypeScript LaTeX transpiler (vscode-chacana/src/server/latex.ts) to Python in src/chacana/. This is Phase 2 of the add-latex-transpiler spec.

title: feat: metric-aware variance tolerance in rank checker (Rule 4)
description:
When an active_metric is defined in the context strategy, the rank checker (Rule 4) should skip variance pattern mismatches. Currently R declared as [Contra,Covar,Covar,Covar] fails when used as R{_a _b _c _d}. With an active metric, implicit index raising/lowering is assumed.

**Files to change:**
- src/chacana/checker.py — _check_rank(): skip variance check when ctx.active_metric is set
- vscode-chacana/src/server/checker.ts — checkRank(): same logic
- docs/assets/js/chacana-checker.js — rebuild after TS change

**Tests (TDD):**
- test_checker.py: R{_a _b _c _d} with active_metric passes (currently fails)
- test_checker.py: R{_a _b _c _d} WITHOUT active_metric still fails (existing behavior)

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Rank count check unchanged (4 indices vs rank 4 still required)
- Variance pattern check skipped when active_metric is present
- All existing tests pass

**Note:** This does NOT handle rank polymorphism (R used at ranks 0/2/4 in Gauss-Bonnet). That requires a separate ticket for implicit contraction support.
Ref: openspec/specs/dsl-specification/spec.md Rule 4, Rule 1 (active_metric)
close_reason: Implemented: variance pattern check skipped when active_metric is set, rank count unchanged

title: feat: support index attachment to parenthesized expressions
description: The PEG grammar and AST currently do not support attaching indices or derivatives to parenthesized expressions (e.g., '(A+B){_a}'). This is required by the differential-geometry/spec.md and peg-grammar/spec.md. Update grammar.py, visitor.py, ast.py, and tree-sitter-chacana/grammar.js to support this.
close_reason: Implemented in PEG grammar, tree-sitter grammar, and added tests

title: fix: VS Code extension build/lint error TS1479
description: The VS Code LSP server fails linting/build with TS1479 because it tries to import the ESM-only 'smol-toml' from a CommonJS context. Fix by adding 'type': 'module' to vscode-chacana/package.json and ensuring relative imports use extensions.

title: feat: implement division operator and rational number reduction
description: The PEG grammar and checker currently lack support for the division operator (/) and rational numbers, which are mentioned in the peg-grammar/spec.md specification (Requirement: Canonical Rational Representation). Implement the / operator in grammar.py, visitor.py, ast.py, and the corresponding logic for rational sign hoisting and reduction.
close_reason: Implemented division operator and rational reduction with sign hoisting and GCD

title: refactor: tighten identifier Unicode range to actual Greek letters used in physics
description:
The identifier regex `[a-zA-Z\u0370-\u03FF]` accepts the entire Greek and Coptic Unicode block, including unassigned code points, archaic letters, and Coptic characters. This creates visual spoofing risk (U+037E Greek question mark looks like semicolon).

Replace with explicit ranges for the characters actually used in tensor calculus:
- Greek uppercase: \u0391-\u03A9 (Alpha-Omega, skipping \u03A2 which is unassigned)
- Greek lowercase: \u03B1-\u03C9 (alpha-omega)

This should be synchronized across:
1. tree-sitter-chacana/grammar.js (identifier rule, line 167)
2. src/chacana/grammar.py (identifier regex and _ALLOWED_LETTER_RE)
3. vscode-chacana/src/server/ast.ts (GREEK_RE, line 88)
4. src/chacana/visitor.py (_greek_lower range, line 49-53)

The Greek character set should be defined as a constant in ONE place and derived elsewhere.

Files: tree-sitter-chacana/grammar.js, src/chacana/grammar.py, vscode-chacana/src/server/ast.ts, src/chacana/visitor.py
Subsumes: chacana-pep

title: refactor: add _sym_index_element rule to tree-sitter grammar to reject nested symmetrization
description:
The tree-sitter grammar allows nested symmetrization because `_index_element` includes `symmetrization` and `anti_symmetrization`, and the `index_list` inside those uses `_index_element`. This means `T{_( _( a b _) _)}` parses without error, violating the PEG spec.

The Python PEG grammar rejects this via post-parse validation (`_reject_nested_symmetrization` in grammar.py). The tree-sitter grammar should reject it structurally:

1. Create `_sym_index_element` that only allows `$.index` (no symmetrization/anti_symmetrization)
2. Create `sym_index_list` that uses `repeat1($._sym_index_element)`
3. Change `symmetrization` and `anti_symmetrization` to use `$.sym_index_list` instead of `$.index_list`

This makes nested symmetrization a parse error rather than requiring a post-parse check.

Files: tree-sitter-chacana/grammar.js, tree-sitter-chacana/test/corpus/ (add rejection test)
Subsumes: chacana-pif

title: refactor: restrict functional_op to known operator keywords in tree-sitter grammar
description:
Currently any identifier(...) is parsed as a functional_op (grammar.js:57-67), meaning R(A+B) becomes a function call instead of implicit multiplication. This is because functional_op uses generic $.identifier and appears before tensor_expr in the _primary choice.

Fix by replacing $.identifier in functional_op with an inline choice of known operator names:
```javascript
functional_op: $ => prec(4, seq(
  field('name', choice('d', 'L', 'Tr', 'det', 'inv', 'star', 'hodge', 'i')),
  '(',
  ...
)),
```

This makes the grammar self-documenting and resolves the ambiguity. Unknown names followed by ( will be parsed as tensor_expr followed by paren_expression (implicit multiplication if the grammar supports it, or a parse error prompting the user to add *).

Files: tree-sitter-chacana/grammar.js, tree-sitter-chacana/test/corpus/ (update tests)
Subsumes: chacana-ghu
Note: Coordinate with operator registry refactoring — the keyword list should come from a single source.

title: refactor: redesign LSP tree storage — one tree per document with incremental updates
description:
The current LSP server has a broken tree storage design:
1. Trees stored with keys ${doc.uri}:${lineIdx} (server.ts:85) — one tree per line
2. Cleanup uses trees.delete(event.document.uri) (server.ts:67) — wrong key format, never matches
3. Trees are never reused — hover and definition re-parse each line independently
4. Each document change creates N new tree objects (one per line)

Redesign to:
1. Store ONE tree per document URI in the trees map
2. Use tree-sitter's incremental parsing (pass oldTree to parse()) for efficient re-parsing
3. Share the tree across diagnostics, hover, and definition providers
4. Delete correctly on document close

This also enables future multi-line expression support (chacana-icb) since the whole document is parsed as one tree.

Files: vscode-chacana/src/server/server.ts, vscode-chacana/src/server/parser.ts
Subsumes: chacana-w6t (memory leak + wrong cleanup key)
Enables: chacana-icb (multi-line expressions, future work)

title: refactor: consolidate test fixtures — move _make_token and diffgeom contexts to conftest.py, create TS fixtures.ts
description:
Test infrastructure has significant duplication:

Python:
- _make_token() is defined identically in test_checker.py:12 AND test_diffgeom.py:19. Move to conftest.py.
- diffgeom_context (48 lines) and no_metric_context (35 lines) are defined locally in test_diffgeom.py. Move to conftest.py for reuse.
- 38+ inline ValidationToken(...) constructions in test_checker.py could use _make_token() instead.

TypeScript:
- 5 test files each have identical beforeAll(async () => { await initParser(); parser = createParser(); }) blocks. Create test/unit/setupParser.ts.
- 4 test files define their own basicCtx/metricCtx/diffgeomCtx inline. Create test/unit/fixtures.ts.
- No shared test helpers exist. Create test/unit/helpers.ts with common patterns.

Files: tests/conftest.py, tests/test_checker.py, tests/test_diffgeom.py, vscode-chacana/test/unit/ (new files)

title: refactor: centralize operator constraint registry to replace 3 scattered maps and 7 if-blocks
description:
Functional operator information is scattered across 3 locations:
1. ast.ts:58-67 / visitor.py:181-190: FUNCTIONAL_OP_MAP / head_map (name → head constant)
2. hover.ts:12-21: OPERATOR_DOCS (name → markdown documentation)
3. checker.py:298-345 / checker.ts:332-409: 7 independent if-blocks in _check_operators/checkOperators

Each operator (d, L, Tr, det, inv, star, hodge, i) has: a name, a head constant, documentation, and validation constraints. These should be unified into a single operator registry:

```python
@dataclass
class OperatorDef:
    name: str
    head: str
    doc: str
    constraints: list[Constraint]  # e.g., RequiresMetric, FirstArgIsVector, MinRank(2)
```

The _check_operators function becomes a loop over the registry instead of 7 if-blocks. Adding a new operator requires adding one entry, not editing 3 files.

Files: src/chacana/ast.py (or new operators.py), src/chacana/checker.py, src/chacana/visitor.py, vscode-chacana/src/server/ast.ts, vscode-chacana/src/server/checker.ts, vscode-chacana/src/server/hover.ts, vscode-chacana/src/server/astBuilder.ts

title: refactor: unify symmetry validation between explicit metadata and declared context
description:
`_check_symmetry` in checker.py has two nearly identical validation blocks:
1. Lines 176-196: Validates explicit groups from `token.metadata["symmetrized_groups"]`
2. Lines 198-220: Validates declared groups from `ctx.tensors[name].symmetries`

Both iterate over position groups and check variance/index_type match with identical error messages. Extract a shared `_validate_symmetry_group(token, group_positions, kind, source_label)` helper that both paths call.

Files: src/chacana/checker.py, vscode-chacana/src/server/checker.ts
Blocked-by: The typed TokenMetadata refactoring (task 2) should land first

title: Wrong repository URL in tree-sitter-chacana/package.json
description: tree-sitter-chacana/package.json:16 has repository.url set to https://github.com/sk/chacana which is wrong. Should be the actual GitHub URL. npm registry metadata and GitHub linking would point to a nonexistent repo.

title: Playground CDN script loaded without Subresource Integrity (SRI) hash
description: docs/playground.md:153 dynamically loads tree-sitter.js from cdn.jsdelivr.net without an integrity attribute. If the CDN is compromised, arbitrary JS executes in users' browsers on the docs domain. Consider self-hosting the file alongside the WASM or using SRI.

title: All 'without context' operator tests are tautologically true
description: Seven tests in test_diffgeom.py (test_hodge_without_any_context at line 216, and similar tests for other operators) call `check(token)` with no context and expect no error. But `_check_operators` only runs when `ctx is not None` (checker.py:363), so these tests verify that skipping all checks produces no error — which is trivially true. They provide zero assurance about code behavior.

title: test_trace_covariant_only_fails has empty pass body — tests nothing
description: test_diffgeom.py:304-308 has only `pass` and a comment saying "covered by no_metric test below." But there is no no_metric test for Trace. The test for `Tr(g{_a _b})` without a metric context does not exist anywhere in the test suite.

title: Checker 'should not raise' tests have zero positive assertions
description: Tests like test_valid_contraction, test_matching_free_indices, test_correct_rank (test_checker.py) only call `check(token, ctx)` and rely on "no exception = pass." Cannot distinguish between "correctly validated" and "silently ignored." A bug causing the checker to short-circuit and skip all rules would leave all these tests green.

title: Grammar acceptance tests only assert result is not None — tautological
description: Every test in TestGrammarAccepts (20+ tests in test_grammar.py) only checks `assert result is not None`. Arpeggio never returns None on success, so this is tautological. A grammar bug that incorrectly groups operators or misparses symmetrization would never be caught. Tests should assert on parse tree structure (rule names, children count).

title: Identifier regex accepts entire U+0370-03FF block including unassigned/Coptic characters
description: The identifier regex [a-zA-Z\u0370-\u03FF] (grammar.js:167) includes unassigned code points, reserved characters, and Coptic letters that aren't Greek. U+037E (Greek question mark, looks like semicolon) could cause visual spoofing. Should be tightened to actual Greek letters used in physics (e.g., \u0391-\u03C9).

title: Mismatched variance markers in symmetrization silently accepted
description: Grammar allows T{_( a b ^)} — covariant opening with contravariant closing. This parses with no error. Mathematical convention and the spec require opening/closing variance markers to match. Should be enforced in the grammar or external scanner.
notes: Ref: openspec/specs/peg-grammar/spec.md — variance marker matching requirement

title: Any identifier(...) parsed as functional_op — no implicit tensor multiplication with parenthesized expression
description: Because functional_op appears before tensor_expr in _primary and matches identifier '(' ... ')', R(A+B) is always parsed as a function call, never as tensor R times parenthesized (A+B). Only known operator names (d, L, Tr, det, inv, star, hodge, i) should match as functional_op. Consider restricting via word property or keyword list.
notes: FIX DIRECTION: Restrict functional_op to match only known operator keywords (d, L, Tr, det, inv, star, hodge, i) using an inline choice instead of generic identifier. Do NOT use word property as it affects the whole grammar.

title: paren_expression with no named children crashes buildAST
description: At astBuilder.ts:79, buildAST(node.namedChildren[0]) is called for paren_expression. If the node has no named children (empty parens or error recovery), namedChildren[0] is undefined, and buildAST(undefined) crashes on accessing undefined.type at line 53 since buildAST has no null guard.

title: TOML context cache never invalidated for out-of-workspace files
description: contextResolver.ts:91 caches by tomlPath with no modification timestamp. Cache invalidation depends on client watching **/*.toml files, but workspace.createFileSystemWatcher only fires for files within the workspace. Context files referenced via absolute paths outside the workspace (e.g., # context: /shared/physics.toml) serve permanently stale data until LSP restart.

title: Negate node handling in _check_contraction relies on accidental fallthrough
description: _check_contraction (checker.py:92-124) has no explicit HEAD_NEGATE case. It works only because the catch-all "elif token.head == HEAD_ADD or token.args:" happens to match Negate (which has args). Refactoring that condition would silently break Negate checking.

title: Functional op with symmetrized indices sets token.indices to tuple instead of list
description: In visitor.py:100-106, visit_factor does token.indices = children[1]. When the functional op has symmetrized indices (e.g., d(omega){_( a b _)}), visit_index_list returns a tuple (list[ChacanaIndex], dict). This tuple gets assigned to token.indices, but downstream code expects list[ChacanaIndex], causing type errors.

title: Wedge product indices collected but never validated in contraction check
description: In _check_contraction (checker.py:94-98), when token.head is HEAD_WEDGE, indices from all args are collected. But only HEAD_MULTIPLY enters the by_label counting/validation block (line 100). Wedge indices are collected and discarded, meaning triple-index or type-mismatch errors in wedge products go undetected.
close_reason: Extracted IndexAnalyzer, fixed all three bugs in commit 1a1da30

title: _check_symmetry accesses indices by metadata position without bounds checking
description: At checker.py:182-183, explicit symmetry metadata positions are used to index into token.indices without verifying they're in bounds. A token with 2 indices but metadata={"symmetrized_groups": [[0, 5]]} crashes with IndexError. The declared symmetry check (line 202) has bounds checking, but the explicit check does not.

title: load_context string-vs-file heuristic allows unintended file reads
description: At context.py:122, when source is a str, it checks "\n" not in source and Path(source).is_file(). Any single-line string matching an existing file path is silently read as a file. load_context("/etc/hostname") would open and try to parse /etc/hostname as TOML. Applications exposing load_context to user input risk file system probing.

title: Global _cached_parser creates race conditions in multi-threaded use
description: _get_parser() (grammar.py:151-159) uses a bare global _cached_parser with check-then-write pattern. In multi-threaded environments, two threads can both create parsers, and sharing a single Arpeggio parser instance across concurrent parse() calls corrupts internal parser state.
notes: FIX DIRECTION: Use threading.Lock around the check-then-write pattern in _get_parser(). Parser creation is expensive so per-call creation is not ideal. threading.local() is another option if concurrent parsing is needed.

title: Implement Chacana LSP server for real-time editor diagnostics
description: Build a Language Server Protocol server that provides real-time diagnostics for Chacana expressions (.chcn files). The LSP combines tree-sitter incremental parsing (tree-sitter-chacana WASM/C) with the static type checker to deliver: (1) syntax error highlighting as-you-type via tree-sitter error recovery nodes, (2) index variance mismatch diagnostics via validation.scm queries, (3) rank/pattern errors by cross-referencing the TOML GlobalContext, (4) go-to-definition for tensor names (jump to TOML declaration), (5) hover info showing tensor rank, index pattern, and symmetries. Architecture: tree-sitter for incremental parse → S-expression queries for fast syntactic diagnostics → full checker pass on save for semantic diagnostics. File extension: .chcn. Should work with any LSP-compatible editor (VS Code, Neovim, Helix, Emacs, Zed). Depends on: tree-sitter-chacana grammar (done), Python checker (done), TOML context loader (done).
notes: Decision: TypeScript implementation. Uses web-tree-sitter with existing WASM binary. Ships as VS Code extension with bundled LSP. Checker rules (~365 LOC Python) to be ported to TS. TextMate grammar (chacana-lfx) folded into same extension package.
close_reason: Complete LSP server implemented in TypeScript. Syntax diagnostics, type checking (5 rules), hover (tensor info + operator docs), go-to-definition (tensor -> TOML), context resolution (directive + sibling walk), TOML file watching. 77 passing tests. Browser checker bundle powers the static playground. Commit 38ba2e2.

title: Implement YachayDB Julia query interface
description: Implement YachayDB <: AbstractInvariantDB with methods: query_invariants, query_identities, lookup_perm, query_rules. Loads from TOML files. Filters by tensor_type, degree, case, dimension.

title: Migrate InvarDB to Yachay format
description: Convert InvarDB from sxAct (Maple cycle notation + Mathematica rules) to Yachay TOML (images notation + rational coefficients). Permutation bases (Step 1) and reduction rules (Steps 2-6). Verify against original using Elegua 3-tier comparison.

title: Implement Yachay TOML schema validator
description: Validate Yachay TOML files: reject non-Chacana expression syntax, validate permutations (no repeats, range [1,N]), check acyclic reduction rules, validate rational coefficients (no zero denominators, no floats).

title: Define Yachay TOML schemas for identities, invariants, and reduction rules
description: Implement the three TOML schemas from yachay-data-model/spec.md Design Details. Identities: tensor_type, class, statement (Chacana micro-syntax), applies_when, verification. Invariants: degree, case, index, independent, permutation. Reduction rules: dependent_index, terms with rational coefficients.

title: Implement AD Emitter surface (compile to differentiable function)
description: compile(session, expression) -> Julia function f(params...) differentiable by Mooncake.jl/ForwardDiff.jl. Single reverse-mode AD pass for gradients. Exploit symmetry for independent components only.

title: Implement AD Consumer surface (Christoffel via DifferentiationInterface.jl)
description: Compute Christoffel symbols by AD of metric components via DifferentiationInterface.jl, not symbolic formulae. Riemann tensor by differentiating Christoffel symbols.

title: Implement coordinate-level symbolic computation layer
description: Implement abstract-to-component lowering in Tequitl.Geom via Symbolics.jl. Translate Chacana abstract index expressions to coordinate-level Symbolics.Num expressions. Exploit declared symmetries to skip redundant components.

title: Port Butler-Portugal canonicalization to Tequitl.Perm
description: Port Butler-Portugal from xAct.jl XPerm as first AbstractCanonicalizer implementation. Must pass the 82-example Butler benchmark suite. No eval(), type-stable.

title: Define abstract trait types with method signatures
description: Implement AbstractCanonicalizer, AbstractSimplifier, AbstractConnection, AbstractInvariantDB with the method signatures from tequitl-architecture/spec.md Design Details. Types defined in Tequitl.Core, MetricDecl in Tequitl.Tensor.

title: Implement Session{S} type with GlobalContext ingestion
description: Implement parametric Session{S} (S=scalar type) that ingests Chacana GlobalContext. Type-stable, no eval(), no module-level mutable state. Validates context at construction time. juliac-compatible.

title: Scaffold tequitl Julia package structure
description: Create tequitl/Project.toml with module layering: Tequitl.Core -> Tequitl.Perm -> Tequitl.Tensor -> Tequitl.Geom -> Tequitl.AD. Enforce dependency ordering. No Symbolics.jl in Core/Perm/Tensor layers.

title: Write processor conformance test suite
description: Create TOML-based test cases for processor conformance: input expression + expected ProcessorResult properties per operation level (Level 0: contract/canonicalize, Level 1: CovD/perturbation, Level 2: AD). Tests run against any processor implementing the interface.

title: Define ProcessorResult JSON schema validation
description: Write validation tests confirming the ProcessorResult TypeScript interface from processor-interface/spec.md. Test schema matches for ok/error status, canonical_hash, symmetry_group, sparsity_pattern fields.

title: Publish chacana package to PyPI
description: Publish chacana as an installable Python package (pip install chacana) so sxAct can consume it as a dependency. The package metadata in pyproject.toml is ready (hatchling build, version 0.1.0). Requires: stable DSL spec and PEG grammar spec. Cross-ref: sxAct translate integration (sxAct-u834) and future ChacanaAdapter both depend on this package being available.
notes: Deferred: PyPI publish is not blocking implementation work. Tequitl and Yachay are in the same monorepo so they don't need a published package. sxAct integration (sxAct-24k5) can use a path dependency or git dependency until a release is warranted. Will publish when the package has processors + a stable API worth distributing.

title: Stabilize foundations RFC (Elegua + Chacana architecture)
description: Finalize the Elegua + Chacana foundations RFC from DRAFT to STABLE. Documents the architectural decoupling strategy: Chacana owns notation/types, Elegua owns orchestration, sxAct owns computation backends. Cross-ref: sxAct-rvzo (refactor sxAct to consume Elegua externally) depends on this.
notes: Foundations RFC now updated: Tequitl named as Tier 3, processor interface contract referenced. See openspec/specs/foundations-rfc/spec.md.
close_reason: Foundations RFC stabilized to v1.0.0: Tequitl naming confirmed for Tier 3, cross-references to processor-interface and tequitl-architecture specs verified correct.

title: Stabilize differential geometry operators spec
description: Move differential geometry operators specification from DRAFT to STABLE. Implementation is complete (checker.py handles d, L, Tr, det, inv, star, i operators with full test coverage in test_diffgeom.py). Scope: review spec against implementation, resolve any gaps, remove DRAFT.
close_reason: Diffgeom spec stabilized to v1.0.0: 4 new scenarios (interior product non-vector, Lie derivative covariant arg, trace rank-deficient, det non-rank-2), Hodge star syntax aligned to hodge()/star(). All 45 tests pass.

title: TASK: Implement MathJSON-compatible AST Generator
description: Implement the generator to transform parsed expressions into a MathJSON-compatible canonical JSON AST. MUST follow the 'ValidationToken' schema defined in openspec/specs/dsl-specification/spec.md.
close_reason: AST aligned with spec: label field, type:TensorExpression, IndexType. Full visitor coverage with 13 new tests. 140 total.

title: TASK: Implement Tree-sitter Grammar for IDE support
description: Implement a Tree-sitter grammar for the Chacana micro-syntax. MUST include S-expression queries for real-time validation of tensor indices and variance matching in symmetrization blocks. Ref: openspec/specs/treesitter-grammar/spec.md
close_reason: Implemented tree-sitter-chacana grammar with 94 tests, external scanner, highlight/validation queries, TOML injection queries, and CI job. Commit c5316dc.

title: Playground: add URL hash state for shareable expressions
description: Persist the current expression and context in the URL hash/params so users can share playground links. E.g. `/playground/#expr=R{^a _b}&ctx=...`. This enables linking from docs examples directly into the playground.

title: docs: add TL;DR blocks and semantically stronger headings across key pages
description:
Source: Documentation review 2026-04-23 ([EXCL-001], [EXCL-002], [EPPO-003]).

Traceability:
- Derived from the 2026-04-23 documentation review recorded in wai research.

Context:
- Key docs lack answer-first TL;DR blocks and some headings are too generic for search/RAG.
- This ticket is intentionally narrowed to avoid overlap with quickstart, troubleshooting, and editor-support tickets.

Files:
- docs/development.md
- docs/playground.md
- docs/guide/context.md
- docs/guide/expressions.md

Acceptance criteria:
- Add concise TL;DR/prerequisites blocks to the listed pages
- Replace weak/generic headings with more semantically distinctive headings where helpful
- Clarify ambiguous setup wording in development.md (especially git hooks/setup expectations)
- Preserve correct Diataxis intent for each page while improving AI retrieval/scannability
- Do not modify quickstart, type-checking, differential-geometry, or editor-support scope covered by other tickets
- Docs build passes

Verification:
- `uv run mkdocs build`
- Manually verify each listed page has answer-first framing and more specific headings where needed
- Manually verify development.md setup wording is unambiguous

Workflow mandate:
- Define the retrieval/scanning failure first, then revise headers/openers, then do style-only cleanup in a tidy pass.

title: feat: rank polymorphism for contracted tensors (Ricci from Riemann)
description:
Allow a tensor declared at rank N to be used with fewer indices when an active_metric is present, interpreting the missing indices as implicit contractions. Example: R declared as rank 4 (Riemann) used as R{_a _b} (Ricci, rank 2) or R (scalar curvature, rank 0).

This is needed for the Gauss-Bonnet invariant: R * R - 4 * R{_a _b} * R{^a ^b} + R{_a _b _c _d} * R{^a ^b ^c ^d}

**Complexity:** High — requires understanding which contractions are valid (e.g., rank 4 → rank 2 via trace is valid, but rank 4 → rank 3 is not standard). May need a contraction_rules declaration in the context.

**Depends on:** chacana-0l0 (variance tolerance)
Ref: openspec/specs/dsl-specification/spec.md Rule 4
close_reason: Implemented rank polymorphism: even-diff implicit traces with active_metric

title: fix: install missing Vale styles and configure linter
description: The prose linter (vale) fails because the 'Google' style is not installed in '.vale/styles/'. Run 'vale sync' to download required packages and ensure they are tracked or correctly ignored if they should be downloaded during setup.

title: refactor: unify type-checking logic between Python and TypeScript
description: The static type-checking logic is currently duplicated between the Python core (src/chacana/checker.py) and the TypeScript LSP server (vscode-chacana/src/server/checker.ts). This leads to maintenance drift. Investigate and implement a unified core, potentially via a shared WASM module or a single source of truth for rules.

title: refactor: strengthen grammar and checker test assertions
description:
Two categories of weak tests identified:

1. Grammar acceptance tests (test_grammar.py TestGrammarAccepts, 20+ tests): All only assert `result is not None`, which is tautological since Arpeggio never returns None on success. Add structural assertions on the parse tree (rule names, children count, operator structure).

2. Checker "should not raise" tests (test_checker.py, 10+ tests): Only call `check(token, ctx)` and rely on "no exception = pass". Add positive assertions:
   - Assert check() returns the token (not None)
   - Assert the returned token's structure is preserved
   - For specific cases, assert that free_indices computation produces the expected result

Also fix test_trace_covariant_only_fails (test_diffgeom.py:304-308) which has an empty `pass` body.

Files: tests/test_grammar.py, tests/test_checker.py, tests/test_diffgeom.py
Subsumes: chacana-ggl, chacana-gv8, chacana-445

title: refactor: add load_context overloads to separate file path from string content
description:
`load_context` (context.py:114-126) uses a heuristic to distinguish file paths from TOML strings: `"\n" not in source and Path(source).is_file()`. This is fragile and a security concern — single-line strings matching existing file paths are silently read as files.

Replace the single `load_context(source: str | Path)` with explicit entry points:

```python
def load_context_file(path: str | Path) -> GlobalContext:
    """Load context from a TOML file."""

def load_context_string(toml_string: str) -> GlobalContext:
    """Load context from a TOML string."""

def load_context(source: str | Path) -> GlobalContext:
    """Load context (auto-detect). Deprecated: prefer explicit variants."""
```

Keep the original for backwards compatibility but add a deprecation warning when the heuristic fires. This makes the API unambiguous and eliminates the file-probing security issue.

Files: src/chacana/context.py, src/chacana/__init__.py (update __all__), vscode-chacana/src/server/context.ts
Subsumes: chacana-88q

title: refactor: replace assert statements with proper validation in visitor.py
description:
The visitor uses Python `assert` statements for validation (e.g., `assert isinstance(idx_node, str)` at visitor.py:172). Under `-O` optimization, assertions are stripped, causing obscure crashes instead of clear errors.

Replace all `assert` statements in visitor.py with explicit `if not ...: raise ChacanaParseError(...)` checks. Also add bounds checking on `children[1]` access in `visit_symmetrization` and `visit_anti_symmetrization`.

While there, also fix visit_factor (line 100-106) to properly unpack the tuple from visit_index_list when symmetrization metadata is present, matching how visit_tensor_expr does it (line 113-117).

Files: src/chacana/visitor.py
Subsumes: chacana-1pq (assert stripped), chacana-356 (children[1] bounds), partially chacana-nen (tuple unpack in visit_factor)

title: CI does not run docs build — broken API references not caught until merge
description: ci.yml runs lint, format, type check, and tests but never runs mkdocs build --strict. docs.yml only triggers on push to main, not on PRs. A PR renaming a function documented via mkdocstrings would only fail after merging.

title: Root .gitignore missing node_modules/ entry
description: The root .gitignore does not include node_modules/. The directory exists untracked. Any git add -A or git add . would accidentally stage it. The vscode-chacana/.gitignore has it but root does not.

title: No tests for _free_indices propagation through functional ops
description: `_free_indices()` falls through to `return list(token.indices)` for HEAD_LIE_DERIVATIVE, HEAD_TRACE, HEAD_DETERMINANT, HEAD_INVERSE, HEAD_HODGE_STAR, HEAD_INTERIOR_PRODUCT, HEAD_PERTURBATION, HEAD_COMMUTATOR. If any of these appear in a sum, free index computation uses the node's own .indices (usually empty) not the free indices of children. No test exercises this.

title: Integer/float split in scalar rule diverges from PEG spec
description: The scalar rule uses choice($.float, $.integer) with separate regexes. PEG spec defines scalar as a single [0-9]+ ('.' [0-9]+)? production. The split means 3. (trailing dot) parses as integer+ERROR instead of a clear scalar error. Consider a single /[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?/ regex.

title: source_file accepts only a single expression — no multi-expression support
description: source_file: $ => $._expression (grammar.js:28) means only one expression per file. Input like 'A + B\nC + D' produces an ERROR for the second expression. If .chcn files should support multiple expressions (one per line), needs repeat() or newline separator.

title: client.start() promise rejection silently swallowed
description: At extension.ts:45, client.start() returns a Thenable that is never awaited or caught. If the WASM file is missing or the server fails to start, the error is silently lost, leaving the extension in a broken state with no user-visible error.

title: IndexType.Spinor defined in enum but never detected by detectIndexType
description: The IndexType enum (ast.ts:11-15) includes Spinor, but detectIndexType (ast.ts:90-92) only returns Latin or Greek. No code path produces IndexType.Spinor, so spinor indices are misclassified as Latin, potentially causing false contraction diagnostics.
close_reason: Spinor detection requires grammar-level index type annotations (future work). Enum value is correctly reserved; no heuristic can reliably distinguish spinor from Latin indices.

title: Race condition on concurrent initParser calls
description: parser.ts:43-58 checks an initialized flag then does async work (TreeSitter.init, Language.load). Concurrent calls both see initialized===false and both proceed. Should store and return a promise to deduplicate.

title: feat: add unary negation support — -T{^a _b} is currently a parse error
description: The grammar rule sum_expr is product_expr, ZeroOrMore(["+", "-"], product_expr), requiring a product_expr first. Leading - for unary negation is not supported, which is a significant expressiveness gap for a mathematical DSL.

title: assert isinstance(idx_node, str) stripped under Python -O flag
description: At visitor.py:172, the assertion validates index node type. With -O optimization, assertions are removed, and if idx_node is None, the code proceeds to _build_index() which crashes obscurely on any(c in _greek_lower for c in name.lower()).

title: visit_symmetrization hard-codes children[1] without bounds checking
description: At visitor.py:152, visit_symmetrization accesses children[1] directly. If children has fewer than 2 elements (grammar edge case), this crashes with IndexError. Same issue in visit_anti_symmetrization (line 156).

title: Create TextMate grammar for VS Code syntax highlighting
description: VS Code does not support tree-sitter natively for custom languages. Create a TextMate grammar (.tmLanguage.json) derived from the tree-sitter grammar.js to provide syntax highlighting in VS Code and other TextMate-compatible editors (Sublime Text, Atom, GitHub linguist). Scope: source.chacana. File extension: .chcn. Should cover all constructs: tensors, indices, variance markers, functional operators, symmetrization/anti-symmetrization, derivatives, perturbation, commutator, scalars, and Greek identifiers. Reference: tree-sitter-chacana/grammar.js for the canonical grammar rules, tree-sitter-chacana/queries/highlights.scm for highlight groupings.
close_reason: TextMate grammar shipped as part of vscode-chacana extension (syntaxes/chacana.tmLanguage.json). Commit 8955c09.

title: CI: verify tequitl module dependency layering
description: CI check that Tequitl.Core imports no downstream modules (Perm, Tensor, Geom, AD) and no Symbolics.jl or DifferentiationInterface.jl. Inspect import/using statements.

title: Validate juliac compatibility (Kretschner scalar tracer bullet)
description: Compile Tequitl public API with juliac. Tracer bullet: Kretschner scalar for Reissner-Nordstrom metric with AD gradients d/dM, d/dQ. Must compile without eval() or type inference errors.

title: Implement AD Oracle surface (sparsity from symmetry)
description: Translate tensor symmetry declarations into sparsity patterns for sparse Jacobian graph coloring. Riemann symmetry: 256->20 independent components. Combine symmetry-derived and structural_zeros sparsity.

title: Playground: add context reset button
description: After modifying tensors in the Context editor, there's no way to restore the default context (R, T, g tensors). Add a 'Reset' button next to 'Copy TOML' that restores the default tensor definitions.

title: README links to contrib/ and queries/ without tree-sitter-chacana/ prefix
description: README.md:75-76 references queries/highlights.scm, queries/validation.scm, and contrib/toml-injections.scm without the tree-sitter-chacana/ directory prefix. These paths don't resolve from the repo root. Users clicking these links from GitHub would get 404s.

title: _parse_variance rejects valid-looking casings like CONTRA
description: At context.py:66-71, only exact strings "Contra"/"contra" and "Covar"/"covar" are accepted. Other casings like "CONTRA", "COVAR" raise ChacanaError. This is a common source of confusion in TOML config files.
